running_variable.name: month
running_variable.description: Calendar month on the running-time axis (epoch 2021-01)

design.action: Berlin Umweltzone stage 2 (1 Jan 2010): a green emissions sticker (Euro-4 diesel / Euro-1 petrol) became mandatory to drive inside the S-Bahn Ring — older non-compliant vehicles banned (not charged).
design.alternative: Roads outside the Umweltzone (outside the S-Bahn Ring): no emissions-sticker requirement.

outcome.name: no2_concentration
outcome.description: Monthly-mean NO2 concentration at the monitoring station

design.estimand: Population effect over the post-intervention window (2010-01 to 2011-12, pre-COVID): the change in in-zone roadside NO2, net of an in-zone urban-background control series sharing the pre-intervention trend.

counterfactual.seasonality: harmonic terms at the 12-month period (1–2 pairs across specs)
counterfactual.justification: The difference of treated and control roadside NO2 nets out the shared regional/meteorological trend and most seasonality; a segmented regression on that difference reads the policy break (level + optional slope) against the extrapolated pre-trend, with residual seasonality carried by harmonic terms and serial correlation handled by Newey-West HAC errors.

control.justification: In-zone urban-background stations share the treated traffic stations' airshed and pre-intervention trend but receive far weaker DIRECT kerbside-traffic treatment, so their series is the counterfactual for the regional/meteorological component of in-zone NO2.

context.description: Berlin BLUME air-quality stations inside the Umweltzone (S-Bahn Ring), split into traffic (treated by the 2010 stage-2 ban) and urban-background (control, far weaker direct kerbside treatment), with a Berlin meteorology join. Data from the EEA historical (AirBase) archive.
context.population: 2 treated + 4 control monitoring stations over 48 analysis months

panel_sample.series_name: in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban (aggregate)
in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban (aggregate)

in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban (aggregate)

no_anticipation.method: no-anticipation placebo break 6 months before the instant (should be ≈0)
no_anticipation.detail: placebo break at 2009-07: 2.556 µg/m³ (se 1.947, assessed=true): meets

control_parallelism.method: pre-period parallel-trends slope of the treated-minus-control difference
control_parallelism.detail: pre-trend slope 0.0019 µg/m³ per month (se 0.1930, n_pre=24): difference is statistically flat; a stable non-zero pre-trend is carried by the segmented model's linear term and validated by the placebo battery

autocorrelation.detail: lag-1 residual autocorrelation 0.139, absorbed by HAC standard errors

its.notes: Controlled ITS: model average over 16 specs (pre-window × harmonics × level/slope × meteorology) on the treated-minus-control monthly NO2 difference, Newey-West HAC (Bartlett lag 4) within each. Honest 95% interval [-6.735, 4.820] (sd 2.925) decomposes into sampling sd 2.637 and identification sd 1.267. Plug-in single-spec interval [-6.622, 1.895] (sd 2.173) is narrower because it ignores between-spec identification uncertainty. Treated = 2 in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban; control = 4 in-zone urban-background stations kept after pre-trend matching (0 dropped: []). Effect is the extra change in treated roadside NO2 net of the control series over 24 post months (2010-01..2011-12). Meteorology-adjusted refit shifts the effect to -1.147 (Δ1.217), confirming the control series already absorbs most short-window dispersion. Checks: control parallelism meets, no-anticipation meets, placebo dates meets. REGIME CAVEAT: Pinned to the Berlin Umweltzone stage 2 (green-sticker Euro-4-diesel BAN inside the S-Bahn Ring). This is a standard-BAN low-emission zone — kept SEPARATE from charge-type zones (London ULEZ) for any future bridge. Germany's 2009 Umweltprämie scrappage scheme falls in the pre-window but is national, so the controlled difference nets it out.

dossier.notes: Controlled ITS: model average over 16 specs (pre-window × harmonics × level/slope × meteorology) on the treated-minus-control monthly NO2 difference, Newey-West HAC (Bartlett lag 4) within each. Honest 95% interval [-6.735, 4.820] (sd 2.925) decomposes into sampling sd 2.637 and identification sd 1.267. Plug-in single-spec interval [-6.622, 1.895] (sd 2.173) is narrower because it ignores between-spec identification uncertainty. Treated = 2 in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban; control = 4 in-zone urban-background stations kept after pre-trend matching (0 dropped: []). Effect is the extra change in treated roadside NO2 net of the control series over 24 post months (2010-01..2011-12). Meteorology-adjusted refit shifts the effect to -1.147 (Δ1.217), confirming the control series already absorbs most short-window dispersion. Checks: control parallelism meets, no-anticipation meets, placebo dates meets. REGIME CAVEAT: Pinned to the Berlin Umweltzone stage 2 (green-sticker Euro-4-diesel BAN inside the S-Bahn Ring). This is a standard-BAN low-emission zone — kept SEPARATE from charge-type zones (London ULEZ) for any future bridge. Germany's 2009 Umweltprämie scrappage scheme falls in the pre-window but is national, so the controlled difference nets it out.

sources.title: Berlin monthly-mean NO2 panel for the Umweltzone stage-2 controlled-ITS (in-zone traffic + in-zone urban-background stations), 2008-01 to 2011-12, from the EEA historical (AirBase) archive
sources.publisher: European Environment Agency (EEA) — Air Quality Download Service (historical/AirBase); measurements reported by Germany (Umweltbundesamt / Berlin Senate BLUME network)
sources.licence: CC BY 4.0 (EEA); air quality measurements reported by Germany (UBA / Berlin Senate)
sources.vintage: EEA historical (AirBase, 2000-2012) air quality, hourly NO2 aggregated to monthly means over 2008-01..2011-12, one row per (station, month). Treated = in-zone (S-Bahn Ring) traffic stations subject to the 2010 Umweltzone stage-2 ban (DEBE065 Frankfurter Allee, DEBE067 Hardenbergplatz); control = in-zone urban-background stations (DEBE010 Wedding, DEBE018 Schoeneberg, DEBE034 Neukoelln, DEBE068 Mitte). Each row carries the monthly-mean NO2 (ug/m3) and the number of valid hours (Validity==1, Value>0; -999 is the missing sentinel).

sources.title: Central-Berlin monthly meteorology (temperature, wind speed + direction components, precipitation) for the Umweltzone controlled-ITS, 2008-01 to 2011-12
sources.publisher: Open-Meteo (ERA5 reanalysis, Copernicus Climate Change Service)
sources.licence: CC BY 4.0 (Open-Meteo); contains modified Copernicus Climate Change Service information (ERA5)
sources.vintage: Open-Meteo historical-weather (ERA5) archive for a central-Berlin point (52.52, 13.405), daily values aggregated to monthly means: temperature_2m_mean (C), wind_speed_10m_mean (km/h), direction-resolved wind components u/v (km/h), precipitation_sum (mm). One row per month over 2008-01..2011-12.

provenance.running_variable_vintage: LAQN ratified hourly NO2 aggregated to monthly means, harvest window 2008-01..2011-12
